description: Section 10: Multi-turn REPL session simulating the example. Iter 1: reasoning sets up full pipeline (dedup + triage via llm_query_batched of 5 records), workers return mixed dispositions including 2 anomalies. Iter 2: reasoning inspects anomalies, generates a cleaning transform via llm_query, applies it, re-triages cleaned records via llm_query_batched. Workers return improved classifications. Iter 3: reasoning sees only 1 remaining anomaly (genuinely novel vendor), routes it to new-vendor intake, generates final battlefield report, returns FINAL.

parts.text: {"record_id": "V-2", "disposition": "anomaly", "confidence": 0.35, "matched_entity_id": null, "rationale": "j&j inc does not clearly match any entity - abbreviated and poorly formatted"}
{"record_id": "V-3", "disposition": "anomaly", "confidence": 0.40, "matched_entity_id": null, "rationale": "ABBOTT labs corp has legal suffixes and inconsistent casing, hard to match"}
{"record_id": "V-4", "disposition": "anomaly", "confidence": 0.20, "matched_entity_id": null, "rationale": "NewVenture BioTech not in known entity list at all"}
{"record_id": "V-5", "disposition": "fuzzy_match", "confidence": 0.72, "matched_entity_id": "E-4", "rationale": "stryker corporation matches Stryker Corporation with case difference"}

args.code:
import re

# Ask LLM to generate a cleaning function
clean_prompt = (
    "Write a Python function: def clean_vendor(record: dict) -> dict\n"
    "It should: 1) Remove legal suffixes (Inc, Corp, LLC, Ltd, PLC) from vendor_name, "
    "2) Expand common abbreviations (j&j -> Johnson & Johnson), "
    "3) Title-case the vendor_name, 4) Title-case the city.\n"
    "Only import re. Return ONLY the Python code."
)
raw_code = llm_query(clean_prompt)

# Safety check and exec
code = raw_code.strip()
if code.startswith("```"):
    code = code.split("\n", 1)[-1].rsplit("```", 1)[0].strip()

for forbidden in ["exec(", "eval(", "os.", "subprocess", "open("]:
    if forbidden in code:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Forbidden: {forbidden}")

sandbox = {}
exec(code, sandbox)
clean_fn = sandbox["clean_vendor"]

# Clean and re-triage anomalies
cleaned_anomalies = [clean_fn(a.copy()) for a in [
    {"id": "V-2", "vendor_name": "j&j  inc.", "city": "new brunswick"},
    {"id": "V-3", "vendor_name": "ABBOTT labs, corp.", "city": "abbott park"},
    {"id": "V-4", "vendor_name": "NewVenture BioTech", "city": "Cambridge"}
]]

for c in cleaned_anomalies:
    print(f"  Cleaned: {c}")

# Re-triage cleaned records
retriage_prompts = [
    f"Triage this CLEANED record: {{record_id, disposition (exact_match|fuzzy_match|anomaly), confidence, matched_entity_id}}.\nEntities:\n{symbol_table}\nRecord: {json.dumps(r)}"
    for r in cleaned_anomalies
]
retriage_raw = llm_query_batched(retriage_prompts)

retriage_decisions = [json.loads(r.strip()) for r in retriage_raw]
retriage_anomalies = [d for d in retriage_decisions if d["disposition"] == "anomaly"]
print(f"Re-triage: {len(retriage_decisions)} classified, {len(retriage_anomalies)} still anomalies")
for d in retriage_decisions:
    print(f"  {d['record_id']}: {d['disposition']} (conf={d['confidence']})")
turn2_remaining_anomalies = retriage_anomalies

parts.text:
import re

def clean_vendor(record: dict) -> dict:
    result = record.copy()
    name = result.get("vendor_name", "")
    # Expand abbreviations
    abbrevs = {"j&j": "Johnson & Johnson", "j & j": "Johnson & Johnson"}
    name_lower = name.lower().strip()
    for abbr, full in abbrevs.items():
        if abbr in name_lower:
            name = full
            break
    # Remove legal suffixes
    name = re.sub(r'\b(Inc\.?|Corp\.?|LLC|Ltd\.?|PLC|SA|AG|GmbH)\b', '', name, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    name = re.sub(r'[,\.]+\s*$', '', name).strip()
    # Title case
    result["vendor_name"] = name.title()
    result["city"] = result.get("city", "").title()
    return result

TURN 3: Final reconciliation. The cleaning transform resolved 2 of 3 anomalies:
- V-2 (j&j inc) -> "Johnson & Johnson" -> exact_match (E-2, conf=0.94)
- V-3 (ABBOTT labs corp) -> "Abbott Laboratories" -> exact_match (E-3, conf=0.91)
- V-4 (NewVenture BioTech) -> still anomaly (conf=0.15) — genuinely novel vendor not in master DB

Final pipeline summary across all 3 turns:
- 5 records processed (0 duplicates)
- Turn 1: 1 exact (V-1), 1 fuzzy (V-5), 3 anomalies (V-2, V-3, V-4)
- Turn 2: Generated cleaning transform, re-triaged 3 anomalies -> 2 resolved to exact_match
- Turn 3: 1 remaining anomaly (V-4) routed to new-vendor intake

Final: 2 exact_match, 1 fuzzy_match from turn 1, 2 exact_match from turn 2, 1 new-vendor.

FINAL(3-turn REPL: 5 records, 4 matched (2 exact + 1 fuzzy + 2 cleaned->exact), 1 new vendor. Transform resolved 2/3 anomalies.)
